unit.name: Hand-built RNN for detecting repeated 1s
unit.description: A worked example manually designs an RNN that detects consecutive ones in a binary sequence.
unit.summary: The concrete example builds an RNN by hand so the forward pass and matrix roles are visible [ts=840s]. The toy many-to-many task takes a binary sequence and outputs 1 only when two 1s occur consecutively [ts=868s] [ts=895s]. To solve it, the hidden state must store the previous input and current x; the example uses a three-dimensional hidden vector for current, previous, and a constant 1, initialized as h_0=(0,0,1) [ts=917s] [ts=934s] [ts=958s]. ReLU is used for simple arithmetic [ts=988s]. W_xh maps x=0 to [0,0,0]^T and x=1 to [1,0,0]^T, W_hh copies the former current value into the previous slot and preserves the constant, and the output computes ReLU(current+previous-1) so only two consecutive ones produce 1 [ts=1069s] [ts=1131s] [ts=1232s].

key_points.text: Toy task: output 1 when the binary input has two consecutive 1s.
The hidden state should capture previous input and current x.
The example hidden vector stores current, previous, and constant 1, initialized as (0,0,1).
W_xh writes the current input value into the hidden state.

kp_catalog.name: Manual RNN state construction
kp_catalog.description: Designing hidden-state contents and weights by reasoning from a toy sequence task.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because manual RNN state construction clarifies designing hidden-state contents and weights by reasoning from a toy sequence task, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Vanilla RNN equations
kp_catalog.description: The tanh-based recurrent update and output projection used as the baseline RNN formulation.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because vanilla RNN equations formalizes how the tanh-based recurrent update and output projection used as the baseline RNN formulation, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
